name: Have No Side Effects
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: A function named for one purpose secretly performs additional state changes (side effects), creating hidden temporal couplings and order dependencies that cause subtle, hard-to-reproduce bugs.
steps: Audit every function for state changes beyond what its name implies.
If a side effect is necessary, rename the function to reveal all of its actions.
Prefer separating the side-effecting operation into its own function called explicitly by the caller.
Avoid modifying global state, session data, or input arguments inside functions that appear to be query-only.
Document any unavoidable side effects prominently and consider making them explicit through the function signature.
